q: What technology do modern stadiums use to track exactly where every player is on the field at all times?
What is 'Hawk-Eye' technology used for in tennis and cricket?
opts: Tracking the exact path of the ball
Video Assistant Referee using camera technology

q: What can AI cameras track about a basketball player during a game?
What does a 'heat map' show coaches after a game?
opts: Areas where players spent the most time

q: How quickly can AI systems measure the speed of a baseball after it is pitched?
opts: They cannot — it moves too fast

q: What is 'pose estimation' used for in sports AI?
opts: Analyzing a player's body position and movement

q: What can AI crowd analytics help stadium managers understand?
opts: How many fans are in each section

q: Why do stadiums need many cameras from different angles instead of just one?
opts: To make the broadcast look nicer
So AI can build an accurate 3D picture of the whole field

q: What does 'goal-line technology' use to decide if a ball fully crossed the line?
opts: A sensor chip inside the ball only
A single camera behind the goal
